rulings.Case Title: MARKEY'S AUDIO VISUAL, INC. A CORPORATION VS. JASON MICHAEL PAUL PRODUCTIONS INC. A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Defualt Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 29, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT JASON MICHAEL PAUL PRODUCTIONS INC.'s Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment. Denied. First, the motion does not comply with CCP sec. 473.5(b), which provides that the motion "shall be accompanied by an affidavit showing under oath that the party's lack of actual notice in time to defend the action was not caused by his or her avoidance of service or excusable neglect" and a proposed responsive pleading. Defendant filed its motion on August 19, 2014, but did not file its affidavit and proposed responsive pleading until September 9, 2014, less than the 16 court days required by CCP sec. 1005. Second, the evidence establishes that Defendant was personally served with the Order for Debtor's Examination on February 8, 2014 and therefore knew about this case on that date. (Declaration of Lorna Walker, par. 12, Ex. C; Declaration of Kris Vorsatz, par. 3.) Defendant did not move to set aside the default and default judgment until August 19, 2014, over six months later. The motion was not brought within a "reasonable time" as required by CCP sec. 473.5(a). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ARTI DENTERLEIN VS. JANE O'HARA GAMP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 29, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANT JANE GAMP, PAT ALLEY, SAN FRANCISCO LAW SCHOOL MOTION / Motion For Protective Order; Proof Of Service. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. The Court finds that the five students at issue have a right of privacy under the California Constitution as to their academic performance and that Plaintiff has not presented sufficient evidence to override these rights. This order does not preclude the five students from voluntarily waiving their privacy rights and producing the information/testimony requested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JASPAL SINGH VS. CHARISMA CRAFTS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn Of Special Motion And Special Motion To Strike (Anti-Slapp Motion)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 29, 2014, Line 9, PLAINTIFF JASPAL SINGH's Special Motion To Strike (Anti-Slapp Motion). The anti-SLAPP motion to strike is granted and denied in part. The commercial exception of CCP 425.17 does not apply to this action. CCP 425.16 does not apply to causes of action 1 and 2. Those causes of action are not based on petitioning activity. They allege that cross-defendant breached the parties' contractual agreement. Therefore, the motion is denied as to causes of action 1 and 2. The first prong of CCP 425.16 applies to causes of action 3 and 4. Cross-complainant demonstrates a probability of prevailing on cause of action 3. Cross-complainant presents evidence indicating that cross-defendant's complaint in this action has no merit and was filed as a means to scuttle the negotiations with Mr. Sheikh. Cross-defendant fails to show that the litigation privilege bars the cause of action. The court grants the motion as to cause of action 4. To prove defamation, cross-complainant must show that cross-defendant uttered the alleged defamatory remarks to a third party. Cross-complainant relies on paragraph 26 of the Rachi declaration. The court sustains cross-defendant's hearsay objection to that portion of the declaration. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MONTEREY INSURANCE COMPANY VS. 1725 FULTON STREET,KLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 29, 2014, Line 10, DEFENDANT STEPHEN BROWN's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The Demurrer is sustained in part and overruled in part. As to the cause of action for rescission, the Demurrer is sustained with ten days leave for Plaintiff to allege the existence of a contract between Plaintiff and Defendant. As to the cause of action for concealment, the Demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts establishing Defendant's duty to disclose. Finally, the Demurrer is overruled as to the indemnity cause of action. Defendant demurred to the cause of action for concealment, but not to the cause of action for intentional misrepresentation, and similarly demurred to the indemnity cause of action solely on the ground that the concealment claim failed to establish a duty. Defendant did not address the indemnity cause of action with respect to the cause of action for intentional misrepresentation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
